description_long: This object is a modern, angular airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a sharply pointed nose. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection, with a slight forward sweep and squared-off tips. The cockpit is enclosed with a large, light blue-tinted canopy positioned at the front of the fuselage. Two engines are mounted under the wings, and the tail section features twin vertical stabilizers with a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is visible beneath the wings and fuselage, suggesting a tricycle configuration. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, suitable for urban or military environments.
description: A sleek, angular airplane with a pointed nose, mid-mounted straight wings, twin underwing engines, and a prominent blue-tinted cockpit canopy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide wingspan, central cockpit canopy, and twin engines beneath the wings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pointed nose, elongated canopy, and the underwing engine placement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the cockpit and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, slightly forward-swept wings and squared tips; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted under the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a large, single-piece canopy; appears to be single or dual-seat.
